title_en: The Baby Died. Whose Fault Is It?

paragraphs.en: Cindy Bi is not supposed to be telling me this story.
paragraphs.zh: 辛迪·比（Cindy Bi）本不该向我讲述这个故事。

paragraphs.en: First, there’s the confidentiality clause. When Bi, a venture capitalist who claims to have invested in a dozen unicorns, hired a surrogate to carry her only male embryo in 2023, both parties agreed to keep the details private and away from the media. Then there’s the restraining order against Bi, followed by a court-ordered agreement saying she would not so much as mention the “surrogate” involved in Baby Leon’s stillbirth. Finally, there are social norms to consider when publicly attacking the woman who says she almost died carrying your child.
paragraphs.zh: 首先是保密条款。2023年，比是一名自称曾投资过十几家独角兽公司的风险投资家。她聘请了一位代孕妈妈来承载她仅有的男性胚胎，双方都同意将细节保密，不让媒体知道。其次有针对比的限制令，随后又有法院命令达成协议，要求她不得提及婴儿莱昂（Baby Leon）死产时涉及的“代孕母亲”。最后，在公开攻击那个几乎在替你怀孕时丧命的女性时，还得顾及社会规范。

paragraphs.en: Still, Bi is talking to me. She sends me a nearly 3,000-item folder filled with legal filings; reports to professional organizations, insurance companies, employers, and the police; emails with her attorneys; and correspondence between her and the “Egg Whisperer” influencer, Dr. Aimee.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，比还是在与我交谈。她给我发来一个近3000项内容的文件夹，里面有法律文件、提交给专业机构、保险公司、雇主和警方的报告、与她律师的邮件往来，以及她与“Egg Whisperer”网红——艾米医生（Dr. Aimee）之间的通信记录。

paragraphs.en: Bi considers herself a whistleblower out to protect “unborn children via surrogacy.” Her website invokes scripture: “Establish justice in the courts. Amos 5:15.” Indeed, Bi has racked up nearly a million dollars in legal bills since 2024, in what she views as a fight to honor her son. “I want the surrogate to be known for what she did, to be set as an example,” Bi tells me. “I hope she goes to jail.” Ideally, for murder.
paragraphs.zh: 比把自己视为揭发人，旨在保护“通过代孕出生的未出生儿童”。她的网站还引用了圣经的话：“在审判中实行公义。阿摩司书 5:15。”确实，自2024年以来，比在这场她认为是为儿子讨公道的诉讼中已花费了接近一百万美元的律师费。“我希望大家知道代孕妈妈所做的事情，让她成为一个反面例子，”比告诉我，“我希望她进监狱。最好是因为谋杀。”

paragraphs.en: American surrogacy is an enormous industry, taking in approximately $5 billion in 2024, and the practice is expected to explode globally almost tenfold in the next decade. It seems especially popular in Silicon Valley, where a growing cadre of investors and executives, from OpenAI’s Sam Altman to Dropbox’s Drew Houston, have used it to grow their families. More than a dozen big tech companies provide five-figure subsidies to any employee who needs or wants to outsource gestational labor. A shocking number of techies now believe growing a baby can be a straightforward business transaction.
paragraphs.zh: 美国的代孕产业规模巨大，2024 年收入约为 50 亿美元，而且这一做法预计在未来十年内在全球范围内大幅增长，可能接近十倍。它在硅谷尤其流行，越来越多的投资者和高管——从 OpenAI 的 Sam Altman 到 Dropbox 的 Drew Houston——都借助代孕来组建家庭。十多家大型科技公司会向任何需要或想要将妊娠劳动外包的员工提供五位数金额的补助。现在，相当多的科技从业者认为，生一个孩子可以像一笔直接的商业交易一样办理。

paragraphs.en: But intended parents and gestational carriers—IPs and GCs, as they’re somewhat dehumanizingly known—are often uninformed about the dearth of regulation and completely unprepared for what can go wrong. Only one state, New York, requires agencies to be licensed. Although America is the world leader in surrogacy, it’s also the developed nation with the highest maternal mortality rate and one of the highest stillbirth rates, a situation described by many as “a public health crisis.” Compared to natural conception, carrying a genetically unrelated fetus more than triples the risk of severe, potentially deadly conditions, a statistic surrogates are rarely given. IPs do not always have to disclose complete medical information, including histories of certain conditions that may harm their GCs. They don’t have to be honest about how many kids they have, why they are hiring a surrogate, or how many other surrogates they have simultaneously pregnant. Do you really know who is carrying your child—or whose child you are carrying?
paragraphs.zh: 但准父母（IP）和孕母（GC）常常对缺乏监管一无所知，也完全没有为可能出现的问题做好准备。只有一个州，即纽约州，要求代孕机构必须持牌。尽管美国是代孕领域的世界领先国家，它也是发达国家中孕产妇死亡率最高的国家之一，也是死产率最高的国家之一，许多人将这种状况称为“公共健康危机”。与自然受孕相比，怀有遗传无关的胎儿会使严重、甚至可能致命并发症的风险提高三倍以上，而代孕者很少被告知这一数据。准父母并不总是必须披露完整的病史，包括可能损害代孕母亲健康的某些病症。他们也不必诚实说明自己已有多少个孩子、雇佣代孕的原因，或同时有多少名代孕母亲在妊娠期。你真的知道是谁在怀着你的孩子——或者说，自己到底是在怀着谁的孩子吗？

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, the US is torn over who controls a pregnant woman’s body. The increasing acceptance of “fetal personhood” means that, in many states, losing a pregnancy can be charged as a felony and potentially punished with life in prison. This is thorny enough when it’s a woman’s own baby. It’s exponentially more fraught when the carrier isn’t the parent.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，美国社会正围绕谁来控制孕妇身体问题不断撕裂。越来越多州开始接纳“胎儿人格”观点，意味着在许多州，流产可能被定为重罪并可能判处终身监禁。即使是自己的孩子流产，这个争议就已非常棘手；但当胎儿的孕育者并非孩子的亲生父母时，这个问题会变得成倍复杂。

paragraphs.en: Many of the issues Bi and her surrogate encountered on their “journey” are likely far more common than you’d ever imagine. But you haven’t heard about them. They won’t influence policy or case law, because they tend to unfold in private, shrouded by confidentiality clauses and handled in closed arbitration proceedings. A stark power differential means that intended parents often have the means to file lawsuits and wage yearslong campaigns, while surrogates who feel screwed are forced to rely on free legal help and GoFundMe.
paragraphs.zh: Bi 和她的代孕母亲在“旅程”中遇到的许多问题，很可能比你想象的更常见。但你从未听说过。它们不会影响政策或判例走向，因为通常都发生在保密条款掩盖下，并在封闭的仲裁程序中处理。巨大的权力差距意味着，准父母常常有能力提起诉讼并发动持续多年的法律攻势，而感到受骗或受损的代孕母亲则只能依赖免费法律援助和 GoFundMe 来维权。

paragraphs.en: The case of Bi and her surrogate shows how, in an environment with little regulation and extreme inequality, the miracle of life can mutate into a death sentence.
paragraphs.zh: 比及其代理孕母的案例表明，在监管稀少且贫富差距极端的环境中，生命的奇迹也可能演变成一场死亡判决。

paragraphs.en: Cindy Bi met her future husband on April Fools’ Day, 2016. It was a warm Friday night at Molly Magees, an Irish pub-slash-discotheque in Mountain View, California. Jorge Valdeiglesias spotted Bi on the dance floor: heart-shaped face, long black hair, snatched size-zero waist. He brought over shots of Don Julio. Bi, a Chinese immigrant and founder who’d later appear on a list of “30 of the Most Successful Early-Stage Startup Investors,” inspected his Google badge to make sure it was real. “I’m older than you,” Bi told him. She was 36 to his boyish, rumpled 28. “But it’s OK, I froze my eggs.” Valdeiglesias was shocked by Bi’s forwardness—he was just looking for a fun night—but it worked.
paragraphs.zh: 2016年4月1日，Cindy Bi在加州山景城一家名为 Molly Magees 的爱尔兰风格酒吧兼舞厅里结识了她的未来丈夫。那是一个温暖的周五夜晚。Jorge Valdeiglesias在舞池里看见了 Bi：心形脸、乌黑长发、纤细得像零号码的腰。她给他上一杯 Don Julio。Bi 是一位中国移民，也是后来会出现在“30位最成功早期阶段创业投资人”名单中的创业者，她先看了他的 Google 工牌以确认身份是真的。“我比你年纪大，”Bi 说，“我比你大36岁到28岁。” 她补充道：“但没关系，我把卵子冻起来了。” Valdeiglesias 对 Bi 的直率感到震惊——他当时只是想找个好玩的夜晚——但这却成了缘分。

paragraphs.en: When she and Valdeiglesias were ready to start a family six years later, surrogacy was the obvious choice. At 43, Bi felt she was too old to be pregnant; she also said she was on a medication that could lead to complications. Bi didn’t grieve the decision. In a Facebook group for future IPs, she wrote: “I’ve been prepared for this for almost a decade.”
paragraphs.zh: 六年后，当她和 Valdeiglesias 准备要孩子时，代孕显然是最明显的选择。43岁的 Bi 觉得自己年龄已经太大，不适合怀孕；她还说自己正在服用一种可能引发并发症的药物。Bi 并未为这个决定感到遗憾。在一个面向未来准父母的 Facebook 群组中，她写道：“我几乎为此做好了十年的准备。”

paragraphs.en: At first, Bi and Valdeiglesias considered implanting two embryos at once to have twins. Bi’s fertility doctor strongly discouraged that because of risks to the babies and surrogate. Instead, Bi and Valdeiglesias settled on “twiblings” gestated by separate women but born a few weeks or months apart. They’d have a protective big brother and a sweet little sister, fortuitously born in the year of the Dragon, the most auspicious sign of the Chinese zodiac.
paragraphs.zh: 最初，Bi 和 Valdeiglesias 曾考虑一次植入两个胚胎，以生育双胞胎。Bi 的生育医生因担心对胎儿和代孕母体的风险，强烈反对了这一做法。后来，两人改为选择“twiblings”——由两位不同的女性孕育、但出生相隔几周或几个月的孩子。这样他们就会有一位有守护力的哥哥和一位可爱的妹妹，而妹妹恰好出生在龙年，这在中国生肖中是最吉利的年份。

paragraphs.en: They signed up with a Southern California–based agency, Surrogate Alternatives Inc. According to their psychological consult, Bi took an SSRI for PTSD spurred by work-related stressors, but hoped to wean off soon. SAI quickly matched the couple with a college-educated bank manager in Virginia. “She was perfect,” Bi told me. “Tall, healthy, young, good job. I showed her off to my friends. The only thing I was concerned with was she’s a single mom, but I saw past it.”
paragraphs.zh: 他们在一家位于南加州的机构“南加州代孕替代方案公司”（Surrogate Alternatives Inc.，简称 SAI）报名。根据他们的心理咨询记录，Bi 因工作压力引发的创伤后应激障碍（PTSD）正在服用一种选择性血清素再摄取抑制剂（SSRI），但希望尽快停药。SAI 很快就把这对夫妇与一位在弗吉尼亚州、受过大学教育的银行经理匹配了。“她非常完美，”Bi 对我说。“个子高、身体健康、年轻、有好工作。我向我的朋友们介绍过她。我唯一担心的是她是单亲妈妈，但我最终看重的是她本身，而不是这个标签。”

paragraphs.en: Rebecca Smith was a former professional athlete who became interested in surrogacy after watching a close friend struggle to conceive. (Although Smith’s real name is public, I’m using a pseudonym to protect her privacy. She declined to be interviewed on the record, citing the confidentiality clause and ongoing litigation.) The 34-year-old wanted to help a family who couldn’t carry their own child. As she put it in a Facebook post, she wanted to give them “the same love I found in becoming a mom.”
paragraphs.zh: Rebecca Smith 是一名前职业运动员，在看到一位亲密朋友长期备孕不顺后，对代孕产生了兴趣。（尽管 Smith 的真名已公开，但为保护她的隐私，我使用了化名。她因保密条款和正在进行的诉讼而拒绝接受实名采访。）这位34岁女性想帮助一个无法自己怀孕的人口家庭。正如她在一篇 Facebook 动态中所写，她想给他们“我在成为妈妈时感受到的同样爱意”。

paragraphs.en: In her profile photos, the pale, lanky Smith mugged in denim jackets and athleisure with her 6-year-old biracial son, whom I’ll call Ellis. Smith had previously dated but told her psych evaluator that “it doesn’t work when you are a single mom.” She spent her free time coaching her son’s sports teams; she ran errands on weekends when Ellis was with his dad, who remained a good friend. She was the type of person who preferred cooking at home to eating out, who made sure to eat breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Ellis was an almost 10-pound baby. The pregnancy had been unremarkable but for the fact, perhaps, that her belly hadn’t shown until she was seven months along.
paragraphs.zh: 在她的资料照片里，这位面色偏白、身形偏瘦的 Smith 穿着牛仔夹克和运动休闲装，与我会称作 Ellis 的6岁混血儿子并排而立。Smith 之前有过恋情，但在心理评估中她说过“单亲妈妈的身份让这件事变得很难”。她空闲时会为儿子的运动队做志愿教练；周末当 Ellis 在爸爸那里时，她会跑腿办事。她属于那种更愿意在家做饭而不是外出就餐的人，而且坚持每天吃早餐、午餐和晚餐。Ellis 是一个将近10磅的婴儿。除了一点小插曲——也许是她的肚子到怀孕第七个月才鼓起——这次怀孕过程并无特别之处。

paragraphs.en: Smith signed up with SAI, a well-regarded agency founded by a surrogate more than two decades earlier. She liked that it offered its GCs numerous safeguards: IPs went through thorough vetting, including psychological screening, and all had a medical reason for pursuing surrogacy; a detailed contract between the GC and the IPs would dictate the terms; a third-party escrow would hold the money and pay Smith; she’d get allowances and reimbursements for housekeeping and maternity clothes; if Smith had to be hospitalized or miss work, she’d receive payments for lost wages and childcare. Also, Smith’s employer-provided insurance would treat the pregnancy as if it were her own baby—a major boon. Although directly paying a woman to carry your child is technically illegal in Virginia, Smith’s contract specified that all payments were “reimbursements.” She planned to use the $45,000 “reimbursement” to pay off her student loans and build an emergency fund.
paragraphs.zh: Smith 在 SAI 报名。SAI 是一家由代孕妈妈二十多年前创办并享有良好口碑的机构。她喜欢的是它为代孕妈妈（GC）设置的多项保障：意向父母（IP）都要经过严格审查，包括心理筛查，而且每一位都必须有选择代孕的医学原因；GC 与 IP 间会签订一份详细合同来规定各项条款；第三方托管账户（escrow）会托管资金并支付给 Smith；她还可以获得家务与产检相关生活用品的补贴和报销；若 Smith 需要住院或因此误工，可获得误工费与托儿费用。除此之外，她的雇主保险会把这次怀孕按“她自己的孩子”来处理，这一点意义重大。虽然在弗吉尼亚州，直接向女性支付代孕费用在法律上是违法的，但 Smith 的合同中写明所有款项都只是“报销”。她计划将45,000美元的“报销金”用于偿还学生贷款并建立应急储备。

paragraphs.en: Smith met Bi and Valdeiglesias in a standard video call facilitated by SAI. In her psychological evaluation, Smith seemed smitten with the couple, calling them “amazing.” The therapist wrote, “She feels they are ‘100% compatible.’” In the summer of 2023, Smith and Ellis flew out to San Francisco for the embryo transfer. Bi put them up in a nice hotel for 12 days, close to her apartment, and planned a detailed sightseeing itinerary. Smith didn’t even request reimbursement for her lost wages.
paragraphs.zh: Smith是在SAI安排的一次标准视频通话中认识Bi和Valdeiglesias的。在她的心理评估中，Smith对这对夫妇几乎一见钟情，称他们“太棒了”。治疗师写道：“她认为他们‘100%契合’。2023年夏天，Smith和Ellis乘机前往旧金山进行胚胎移植。Bi把她们安排在她公寓附近的一家舒适酒店住了12天，并精心计划了详细的观光行程。Smith甚至没有申请补偿自己因此损失的工资。

paragraphs.en: A doctor implanted Bi and Valdeiglesias’ only male embryo inside of Smith. Everyone was elated when it stuck. Bi asked Smith for permission to share a photo of the test result on Facebook, and Smith quickly agreed, inviting Bi to tag her. Bi and Smith texted all the time: Smith shared her happy nausea and impressions of Baby Leon; Bi detailed her constant travels and her search for a second GC. Smith tried to recruit her own sister for the job, then a coworker. When scary but benign early bleeding brought Smith to the ER, Bi sent her a DoorDash gift card.
paragraphs.zh: 一名医生把Bi和Valdeiglesias的唯一一个男性胚胎植入了Smith体内。胚胎着床成功后，大家都十分欣喜。Bi征求Smith是否同意在Facebook上分享验孕结果的照片，Smith很快同意了，并请Bi给她@（标注）身份。Bi和Smith几乎每天都在发信息：Smith会分享自己“幸福的孕吐感受”以及对Baby Leon的第一印象；Bi则详细说明自己频繁出差的情况，并寻找第二位代孕妈妈的进展。Smith先是想让自己妹妹来做这个工作，后来又尝试让一位同事帮忙。当一次看似危险但结果良性的早期出血把Smith送到急诊室时，Bi给她发了一张DoorDash礼品卡。

paragraphs.en: But in the wake of the bleeding, Smith found something out. Bi had been posting about Smith’s health in Facebook groups, sharing test results and crowdsourcing suggestions for her treatment. Bi didn’t refer to Smith by name but included distinctive details that allowed members of the tight-knit community to identify her, which violated their contract.
paragraphs.zh: 但就在出血事件之后，Smith发现了另一件事。Bi一直在Facebook小组里发布关于Smith健康状况的内容，分享验血/检验结果，并向大家征集她治疗方案的建议。Bi虽然没有直接点名Smith，但她提供了足够有辨识度的细节，让这个关系紧密的圈子里的成员能够认出她，这一做法违反了双方的协议。

paragraphs.en: Smith alerted SAI but didn’t confront Bi, perhaps calculating that it was better not to risk tainting their rapport. After all, the surrogacy relationship was unlike almost any other. Once you were pregnant, there was no going back. A new human had been—had to be—created.
paragraphs.zh: Smith向SAI提出了提醒，却没有当面对Bi提出质问，可能是出于谨慎，觉得这样做会有损两人的关系。毕竟，代孕关系几乎与任何其他关系都不同。一旦怀孕，就没有回头路了。一个新生命已经——也不得不——被创造出来了。

paragraphs.en: In late 2023, a little more than halfway through the pregnancy, Bi got a Facebook comment that scared her. She had posted about Smith getting a new, better job; the COO of ART Risk Solutions, an insurance agency, replied, suggesting that Bi double-check whether Smith’s new policy would cover the pregnancy. Outreach like this wasn’t uncommon: The Facebook groups often teemed with industry professionals peddling their wares. The ART Risk COO offered to look into it.
paragraphs.zh: 2023年底，Bi 怀孕进展到略超过一半时，收到了一条吓到她的 Facebook 评论。她发帖说 Smith 找到了一份更新、更好的工作；保险公司 ART Risk Solutions 的首席运营官回复了她，建议她再确认 Smith 的新保单是否会覆盖怀孕期间的保险。此类接触并不罕见：Facebook 群组里经常挤满了到处推销自己服务的行业从业者。ART Risk 的首席运营官还主动表示可以帮她调查。

paragraphs.en: While SAI’s standard process had determined that Smith’s new insurance would cover the pregnancy, ART Risk disagreed. Bi was spooked by what she saw as SAI’s oversight on a major issue: Pregnancy complications could rack up five- or six-figure bills. To Bi, the people she had paid to ensure her only son’s healthy birth seemed asleep at the wheel. Since Bi alleged SAI had “made a mistake,” she demanded they pay for Smith’s backup insurance policy. When it stood by its assessment and refused, Bi was irate.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然 SAI 的标准流程已确认 Smith 的新保险会涵盖怀孕，但 ART Risk 却持不同意见。Bi 被她认为是 SAI 在一个重大问题上的疏忽吓到了：妊娠并发症可能会产生数万到数十万的账单。对 Bi 来说，那些她付钱雇来确保她独子能顺利出生的人，仿佛在开车时打盹。既然 Bi 指称 SAI“弄错了”，她便要求他们支付 Smith 的补充保险。SAI 坚持自己的评估并予以拒绝后，Bi 十分恼怒。

paragraphs.en: She began posting about SAI, sometimes multiple times a day, to warn other IPs. Sometimes she said that Smith had told her about the job change, other times she claimed that Smith hadn’t notified her. She didn’t want to burden Smith. “It’s not your fault,” she assured Smith, trying to protect her from the stress while keeping her in the loop.
paragraphs.zh: 她开始频繁发帖，关于 SAI，有时一天发好几次，目的是提醒其他 IP。她有时说 Smith 已经把工作变动告诉过她，有时又说 Smith 并没有通知她。她并不想给 Smith 增加负担。“这不是你的错，”她向 Smith 保证，尽量减轻她的压力，同时又让她随时了解进展。

paragraphs.en: It seemed like they were on the same page. “I just hate that it all happened!” Smith texted Bi. “I was so excited thinking the new job was surrogate-friendly.” Bi was sure that if Smith had known otherwise, she wouldn’t have taken the job.
paragraphs.zh: 看起来他们似乎在一个频道上。“我只是恨不得这一切都没有发生！”Smith 给 Bi 发短信道。“我当时太兴奋了，因为我以为这份新工作对代孕更友好。”Smith 很肯定，如果她事先知道不是这样，就不会接受这份工作。

paragraphs.en: It wasn’t about the money. Smith’s new, subsidized Affordable Care Act plan, at $391 a month, was a drop in the bucket compared to the estimated $200,000 Bi had budgeted for the pregnancy. Bi told Smith that they would lay low until Leon was born and then go after SAI: “I want to show them what justice should look like.”
paragraphs.zh: 这并不是为了钱。史密斯每月391美元的新补贴《平价医疗法案》方案，与比先前为这次怀孕估算的20万美元预算相比，只是沧海一粟。比告诉史密斯，他们打算等莱昂出生后再对SAI发起行动：“我要让他们看看什么才算正义。”

paragraphs.en: On December 15, a day on which Bi sent her more than 50 texts about the insurance, Smith felt liquid between her legs. She was 26 weeks pregnant and afraid her water had broken. The emergency room sent her home, telling her it wasn’t amniotic fluid. She should’ve been relieved, but she soon had another text from Bi: One of Bi’s lawyers wanted Smith “to sign a few forms.” Smith had already signed a power of attorney giving Bi and Valdeiglesias the ability to make decisions for Leon. Were they now asking for control over her body? (Bi declined multiple requests to show me the forms in question.)
paragraphs.zh: 12月15日，那天比给她发了50多条关于保险的短信，史密斯感觉自己两腿之间有液体。她当时怀孕26周，担心是羊水破了。急诊室把她送回家，并告诉她那不是羊水。她本应松一口气，但不久后又收到一条比发来的信息：比的一位律师想让史密斯“签几份文件”。史密斯早已签署过一份授权书，赋予比和瓦德埃莱西亚斯替莱昂做决定的权力。难道他们现在是在要求控制她的身体吗？（比多次拒绝我查看相关文件。）

paragraphs.en: Smith confronted Bi gently. “I guess I’m a little confused,” she said. Bi and Valdeiglesias hadn’t been calling into any of the prenatal appointments where they could ask questions. “What’s changed?”
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯平静地质问比：“我想我有点不太明白。” 比和瓦德埃莱西亚斯并没有参加过任何一次产检咨询电话，因此也没有在那里提问。 “发生了什么变化？”

paragraphs.en: “After your ER visit, I have tons of questions,” Bi replied. “For example, was it due to the stress of Friday’s insurance discussion? If not, what can we do to prevent such episodes so baby can grow to full term?” By this point, Bi and Valdeiglesias had found a second GC for Leon’s younger sister, and that process, Bi told Smith, was going much more smoothly.
paragraphs.zh: “在你去急诊后，我有很多问题要问，”比回答道。“比如说，是否是因为周五那场保险讨论带来的压力？如果不是，我们还能做些什么来防止再次发生这种情况，让孩子安全足月出生？” 到这时，比和瓦德埃莱西亚斯已经为莱昂的妹妹找到了第二位GC，按比对史密斯说，这个过程进行得顺利得多。

paragraphs.en: That day, Smith was back at the clinic for follow-ups on the leaking. She’d already started taking precautions: sleeping on her left side and drinking a gallon of water a day. Again, they told her it wasn’t amniotic fluid and that stress might have been a contributor.
paragraphs.zh: 那天，Smith回到诊所复查渗漏情况。她已经开始采取预防措施：侧卧在左边，并且每天喝一加仑水。医生再次告诉她那不是羊水，压力可能也是一个诱因。

paragraphs.en: On January 1, 2024, 29 weeks pregnant, Smith texted Bi that she was in the ER again. This time, doctors confirmed that her water had broken. Smith was admitted to the hospital for IV antibiotics, monitoring, and steroids to help develop the baby’s lungs. She was to stay there until Leon was born.
paragraphs.zh: 2024年1月1日，怀孕29周的Smith给Bi发短信说她又去了急诊。此次，医生确认她的“羊水”已经破了。Smith被收治入院，接受静脉抗生素、监测和类固醇治疗，以帮助婴儿肺部发育。她将待在医院里，直到Leon出生。

paragraphs.en: Bi and Valdeiglesias had just celebrated New Year’s Eve in New York City. A few days later, Bi ordered Smith a tower of boxes from Amazon: coconut water, freeze-dried cantaloupe slices. But Bi also pestered Smith with questions: Why was she in the hospital, instead of just on bedrest at home? Was she submitting lost wage requests? Meanwhile, on Facebook, Bi claimed that Smith had broken their contract by not notifying her before changing jobs. A breach claim was serious. It meant that Smith was on the hook for any bills that insurance didn’t cover—potentially million-dollar-plus sums that Bi acknowledged could bankrupt Smith. (Technically, Smith had notified them: She had a text from Valdeiglesias congratulating her.)
paragraphs.zh: Bi和Valdeiglesias刚刚在纽约市度过了新年夜。不久之后，Bi从亚马逊给Smith又订购了一堆包裹：椰子水、冻干哈密瓜片。但Bi也一直追问Smith：她为什么要住院，而不是在家里卧床休息？她是否在申请丢失工资补偿？与此同时，Bi在Facebook上声称，Smith在换工作前没有通知她，已经违背了他们的合同。违约指控很严重。这意味着所有保险不报销的账单都可能由Smith承担——可能是数百万美元以上的金额，Bi也承认这可能让Smith破产。（严格来说，Smith是有通知过她们的：她收到过Valdeiglesias发来的祝贺短信。）

paragraphs.en: The situation reveals one of the fundamental imbalances of surrogacy: When a surrogate breaks contract, her IPs can stop paying her and stop paying the medical bills for her pregnancy. But if an IP breaks contract—say, by sharing their GC’s private information online or withholding compensation—a GC typically has to hire a lawyer. No matter what, IPs get the baby at the end. Anything else would be considered baby selling and human trafficking.
paragraphs.zh: 这个情况揭示了代孕关系中的一种根本失衡：当代孕母违约时，意向父母（IP）可以停止支付她的薪酬，并停止支付她怀孕期间的医疗费用。但如果是IP违约——比如在网络上泄露他们的基因捐赠者（GC）私人信息或拖欠报酬——GC通常必须请律师。无论如何，最后孩子都会落到IP手里。除此之外的任何处理，都可能被视为售婴和人口贩运。

paragraphs.en: Bi started to believe something was wrong with Leon because of photos Smith posted. She looked slim, despite being months along. You hardly look pregnant! a commenter wrote, approvingly. To Bi, this wasn’t a compliment. Everyone knew pregnant women had big bellies.
paragraphs.zh: 比看了史密斯发布的照片后，开始相信莱昂出了问题。她看起来很瘦，尽管已经怀孕了几个月。评论区有人赞许地写道：你看起来都不怎么怀孕。对比来说，这对比莉来说根本不是夸奖。大家都知道，怀孕的女人肚子都会变大。

paragraphs.en: A few days into Smith’s hospitalization, Bi got news that seemed to push her over the edge. Smith phrased it like it was a good thing: Despite the premature rupture of membranes, her doctors had told her that Leon was healthy, and a growth scan estimated that he was in the 30th percentile. That didn’t sound right. The 30th percentile meant most babies measured bigger than Leon. Smith was tall—shouldn’t that make a bigger baby, Bi wondered? She had expected her son to be above average in all things.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯住院几天后，比利得到了一个消息，似乎把她逼到了崩溃边缘。史密斯这样说出来，似乎在讲一件好事：尽管出现了早破，医生仍告诉她莱昂健康，而且一次宫内生长超声显示他处于第30百分位。这个说法听起来不对劲。第30百分位意味着大多数婴儿都比莱昂大。莱昂的母亲比利想，史密斯个子很高——这不应该长出更大的孩子吗？她一直以为儿子在各方面都会高于平均水平。

paragraphs.en: Smith maintained that the doctor told her everything looked great. But Bi’s motherly intuition was going off. Something didn’t seem right with her son. She pressed on: Was Smith eating enough? Had she gained enough weight? “Small belly by itself, I brushed off,” Bi wrote. But “30th, water leak, small belly, is Leon being suffocated and become defective already?”
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯坚持说医生告诉她一切都很好。但比利的母性直觉越来越不对劲。她觉得儿子哪里不对劲。她继续追问：史密斯有没有吃得够？她增重是否足够？“单看肚子小，我当时也不当回事，”比利写道。“但‘第30百分位、羊水漏、肚子小，莱昂是不是已经缺氧而发育不良了？’”

paragraphs.en: Since Bi did not have Leon in her body, where she could protect him and keep him safe, she had to do everything she could from across the country. She turned to Facebook groups and Google for second, third, and fourth opinions. On January 13, Bi emailed SAI asking for copies of Smith’s medical records.
paragraphs.zh: 因为比利身体里并没有莱昂，她没法像平时那样保护他、照看他、让他安全，她只能在全国各地尽她所能。她转向了 Facebook 群组和谷歌，寻找第二、第三、第四种意见。1 月 13 日，比利给 SAI 发邮件，索取了史密斯的病历复印件。

paragraphs.en: Bi—a direct communicator who prided herself on her candor—asked her husband if she was out of line. Valdeiglesias assured her she wasn’t. “You’re just caring for your baby,” he said. “Like just what any other mother would do.”
paragraphs.zh: Bi 是个沟通直接、以坦率为荣的人，她问丈夫自己是否越了界线。Valdeiglesias 让她放心地回答：她没有越界。“你只是在照顾自己的宝宝，”他说，“这正是任何母亲都会做的事。”

paragraphs.en: The hardest part of being hospitalized, Smith told Bi, was saying goodbye to her son, who cried whenever she had to leave. Bi had suggested that Ellis could stay in the hospital. Ellis spent the night just twice in those weeks, sleeping on the recliner meant for new parents.
paragraphs.zh: Smith 对 Bi 说，住院最难受的部分，是和儿子告别：每次她要离开时，儿子都会哭。Bi 曾建议 Ellis 可以留在医院过夜。这些周里 Ellis 只住过两晚，睡在专为新手父母准备的躺椅上。

paragraphs.en: On one of those mornings, January 21, Smith woke up in the same room as her sweet, protective, now 7-year-old son. Nurses came in and did morning monitoring. Leon had no heartbeat.
paragraphs.zh: 在其中一个早晨，1月21日，Smith 与她那位可爱又很护短、现在7岁的儿子在同一个房间里醒来。护士走进来进行早间监测。Leon 没有心跳。

paragraphs.en: When Bi got the Facebook message from an unknown woman, she thought it was a prank: “Call the hospital asap,” the woman wrote. It was Smith’s sister. “This is an emergency.”
paragraphs.zh: 当 Bi 收到一条来自不认识的女子的 Facebook 信息时，她以为那是个恶作剧。那名女子写道：“尽快打电话给医院。”那是 Smith 的姐姐。“这是紧急情况。”

paragraphs.en: Bi recorded her conversation with the hospital. The doctor gave her condolences: She thought she’d seen a flicker in one of Leon’s heart valves and so rushed Smith into an emergency C-section. But Leon was already dead. The placenta had separated from Smith’s uterine wall, depriving Leon of oxygen. “Typically that leads to vaginal bleeding,” the doctor explained, “which she had not been having. She did about 10 days ago.” They’d followed the standard of care by trying to keep Leon inside Smith until 34 weeks gestation. But, she said, “these things just happen sometimes. I don’t think we’ll ever know exactly why, unfortunately.”
paragraphs.zh: Bi 记录了她与医院的通话。医生向她表示慰问：她当时以为在 Leon 的一个心脏瓣膜里看到了搏动的迹象，因此紧急为 Smith 做了剖腹产手术。但 Leon 已经去世。胎盘脱落于 Smith 的子宫壁，导致 Leon 缺氧。医生解释说，这通常会导致阴道出血，而她当时并没有出现这种情况。她大约在十天前有过出血。医生说，他们已经按照标准护理流程，在 34 周妊娠时尽量让 Leon 留在 Smith 的子宫里。然而，医生接着说，像这种事有时会发生。遗憾的是，我们可能永远无法确切知道原因。

paragraphs.en: Later, Bi got a call from Smith, who had recently woken up from general anesthesia. Snotty-nosed, crying, Smith told her she last felt Leon moving that night. Bi was confused: Smith had told her that Leon was moving more during the day and less at night. The call button was right there, Bi thought. If Smith felt Leon move, why hadn’t she alerted the nurses?
paragraphs.zh: 后来，Bi 接到 Smith 的来电。Smith 刚从全身麻醉中醒来，边抽泣边电话里说着。她带着鼻涕说，自己昨晚最后一次感觉到 Leon 在动。Bi 感到很困惑：Smith 曾告诉她，Leon 白天活动得更频繁、夜里活动更少。Bi 心想，导诊铃就在那儿。如果 Smith 能感觉到 Leon 在动，她为什么没有立刻按铃通知护士？

paragraphs.en: Bi and her husband arranged for flights to see their son’s body. She emailed SAI: Could the medical records be ready when she arrived at the hospital? “I didn’t sleep,” Bi said, “I was contacting attorneys.”
paragraphs.zh: Bi 和丈夫安排了航班，要去看他们儿子的遗体。她给 SAI 发了邮件：她到医院时，医疗记录能准备好吗？Bi 说：我一夜没睡，“我一直在联系律师。”

paragraphs.en: Smith, meanwhile, had spent several hours in surgery. Placental abruptions are a major cause of maternal mortality. According to court filings, she “lost a lot of blood and nearly died.”
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，Smith 在手术室里待了好几个小时。胎盘早期剥离是导致孕产妇死亡的重要原因之一。根据法院文件显示，她“失血很多，几乎丧命”。

paragraphs.en: Now awake, sliced through her core, she held baby Leon. He weighed 3 pounds, 12 ounces—almost exactly average for his gestational age. The child that she’d carried for seven months lay dead in her arms.
paragraphs.zh: 此时她已清醒，心口像被利刃切开一般，她抱着刚出生的男婴莱昂。他重3磅12盎司，几乎正好是该孕周的平均体重。这个她怀了七个月的孩子，正躺在她怀里，一动不动。

paragraphs.en: What shocked Bi, when she arrived at the hospital, was the joy she felt with her stillborn child. “He was a white boy, just like his dad.” He had light hair, a cut near his left knee, and hard-looking fingernails. Bi photographed Leon in a white gown and diaper, holding a blue crocheted heart. Bi would never know the color of his eyes.
paragraphs.zh: 比奥第一次到医院时最震惊的是，她居然对这个死产婴儿感到欣喜。“他是个白人男孩，和他爸一模一样。”他有浅色头发，左膝外侧有一道切口，指甲看起来很硬。比奥给莱昂拍了照，莱昂穿着白色长袍和尿布，抱着一个蓝色钩针编织的心形玩偶。比奥永远也不知道他的眼睛是什么颜色。

paragraphs.en: A nurse told Bi that Smith was being discharged but wanted to speak to them. Bi said no; it was her time with her son before his autopsy. She did ask for a list of Smith’s medications.
paragraphs.zh: 一位护士告诉比奥，史密斯将被出院，但他想和他们谈谈。比奥说不。现在是她和儿子最后相处、在验尸前的时间。她只是请求要一份史密斯的用药清单。

paragraphs.en: Eight days after Leon died, Smith emailed Bi and Valdeiglesias. She understood they might not want to talk, but her heart was breaking, thinking of them. “I will forever carry the memory of your baby boy,” she wrote, “how his favorite place to kick/punch was my right rib and how he danced up a storm whenever Ed Sheeran came on.”
paragraphs.zh: 莱昂去世八天后，史密斯给比奥和瓦尔德伊格利西亚斯发了邮件。她明白他们可能不愿意交谈，但想到他们，心就碎了。“我将永远记得你们的儿子，”她写道，“他最喜欢踢、拍打的地方是我的右肋骨，每次艾德·希兰的歌一响起，他就会疯狂地跳起来。”

paragraphs.en: Back home in San Francisco, Bi felt empty. She wandered the sidewalks where she should’ve been pushing a stroller. Things were bad at home. She couldn’t sleep, couldn’t work. Instead she dedicated all of her time to taking screenshots and organizing folders of evidence to wage a medical malpractice lawsuit.
paragraphs.zh: 回到旧金山后，Bi 感到空虚。她在本该推着婴儿车的马路人行道上独自徘徊。家里的一切都很糟。她睡不着，也不能工作。于是她把所有时间都用来截取截图、整理证据文件夹，以此准备提起医疗过失诉讼。

paragraphs.en: She also hired psychics to give her answers. As she tells it, they all blamed Smith. One suggested that an ex-boyfriend of Smith’s had turned her against Baby Leon. Another claimed to see traumas on Smith’s belly and said she was clearly having rough sex. He warned: “She has something to hide.” When Smith refused to release her medical records unless nonpregnancy information was redacted, Bi saw it as confirmation that Smith was hiding crucial details.
paragraphs.zh: 她还去请了通灵人士给她“找答案”。她是这样描述的：这些人都把责任归到 Smith 身上。有人说是 Smith 的一个前男友把她对 Baby Leon 的好感转变了。另一个人声称在 Smith 的肚子上看到了创伤痕迹，并说她明显有过粗暴的性行为。他警告道：“她有一些要隐藏的东西。”当 Smith 拒绝在不把与怀孕无关的信息打码的情况下放出病历时，Bi 把这当作 Smith 隐瞒关键细节的证据。

paragraphs.en: Bi contacted SAI, claiming that Smith had breached contract by not informing her about the insurance change on time, not taking her vitamins, and not alerting her before the C-section. “Our contract specified a ‘well-baby’ that didn’t die,” she reminded them. Bi ordered the escrow to stop paying Smith or reimbursing her medical expenses. A few days later, Bi re-listened to the recording of the worst news of her life. She noticed a detail she’d missed: Smith had bled 10 days prior to Leon’s death. No one had told her.
paragraphs.zh: Bi 联系了 SAI，声称 Smith 违约，因为她没有按时告知保险变更、没有给她提供维生素，并且在剖腹产前没有提前警示。她提醒对方，“我们的合同里明确约定的是‘婴儿平安出生’，不是‘婴儿死亡’。”Bi 要求托管方停止继续向 Smith 付款，也不再报销她的医疗费用。几天后，Bi 再次听了那段人生最坏消息的录音。她发现了一个自己此前没注意到的细节：Smith 在 Leon 去世前 10 天就已经出血。没有人告诉她。

paragraphs.en: SAI countered that there was “no documented bleed” on the date in question but clarified that there was “some light pink fluid which the doctor was not concerned about.” SAI said Smith asked the doctors to tell Bi directly, and that the contract gave Smith two weeks to tell Bi. “That’s emergency information,” Bi said. “She should have told me right away.” If Bi had been told, she believed that Leon would be alive. She would’ve insisted on a C-section immediately.
paragraphs.zh: SAI 反驳称，在相关日期“没有记录到出血”，但也澄清有“少量浅粉色液体”，医生当时并不担心。SAI 说 Smith 要求医生直接告诉 Bi，并且合同给了 Smith 两周时间来告知。Bi 说：“那是紧急信息。她应该立刻告诉我。”如果当时有人告诉她，她相信 Leon 现在还活着。她会立刻要求进行剖腹产。

paragraphs.en: Many stillbirth advocates believe that American doctors do not take the risk of stillbirth seriously, that the standards of care are often woefully inadequate, and that calculations about when to deliver a baby ignore risk factors. After losing a baby, women are often told, as Bi was, that these things “just happen.” Stillbirths take about 21,000 American children per year—more than guns, car accidents, sudden infant death syndrome, cancer, and fires combined.
paragraphs.zh: 许多支持死产问题的倡导者认为，美国医生并不真正重视死产风险，护理标准常常远远不够充分，而且有关何时分娩的计算也忽视了风险因素。许多母亲在失去婴儿后，常被告知，正如Bi被告知的那样，这些情况“就是会发生”。在美国，每年大约有21,000名孩子死于死产——这已超过枪支、交通事故、婴儿猝死综合征、癌症和火灾事故相加的总数。

paragraphs.en: Leon’s death “was 100 percent, 1,000 percent preventable,” Bi told me, anguished. Searching for meaning, she started attending Epic Church (known for hosting high-profile VCs). At one discussion, participants talked about “when God gives you a mission.” Bi sobbed. “I don’t want this mission, I want my son,” she thought. Upon reflection, though, she became hopeful. She thanked God for giving her trauma, because He believed she could handle it. “I will do my best to make sure it won’t happen to other people,” she promised herself. She would become her son’s voice. “I'll make sure other unborn children are protected.”
paragraphs.zh: Bi苦恼地告诉我，Leon“有100%——甚至1000%都可以预防。”为了寻求意义，她开始参加Epic教会（该教会以接待知名风险投资人而闻名）。在一次讨论中，参与者谈到了“上帝给你一种使命”这句话。Bi突然哭了起来：“我不想要这个使命，我想要我的儿子，”她心里想。后来回想时，她却重新燃起了希望。她感谢上帝给了她这场创伤，因为她认为上帝相信她能够承受它。“我会尽力确保这件事不会发生在别人身上，”她对自己许下承诺。她要为她的儿子发声。“我会确保其他未出生的孩子受到保护。”

paragraphs.en: In her grief, Bi pieced together her version of events: During the pregnancy, Smith had engaged in “lots of unsafe sex.” A bout of “forceful sex” led to her leaking in mid-December. More sex eventually necessitated her hospitalization. Smith had let her “adult-sized” son sleep in her bed, where he’d probably kicked her in the stomach. Smith may have even intentionally given birth early, mistakenly believing she’d receive full compensation.
paragraphs.zh: 在悲痛之中，Bi整理出了自己版本的经过：怀孕期间，Smith“进行了大量不安全的性行为”。一次“激烈的性生活”导致她在12月中旬出现漏液。更多的性行为最终让她不得不住院。Smith让“成年人的尺寸”的儿子在她的床上睡觉，这个孩子很可能踢了她的肚子。Smith甚至可能故意提前生产，错误地以为她会因此拿到全部的报酬。

paragraphs.en: At the advice of her lawyer, Bi hired a private investigator. A reverse address lookup suggested a man shared Smith’s address—whose name Bi was sure had to be Smith’s (supposedly ex) partner. He was Black, and the PI’s report described him as having been charged with two misdemeanors. Bi believed Smith should have never been a surrogate with an “undisclosed live-in boyfriend with #felony JAIL TIME record,” as she put it online. The PI dug up a flyer advertising a New Year’s Eve party listing the man as one of the DJs, seemingly confirming Bi’s lawyer’s suspicion about what Smith had been doing the night before her hospitalization.
paragraphs.zh: 在律师建议下，Bi雇了一名私人调查员。反向地址查询显示有一名男子与Smith共享地址——Bi确信那人一定是Smith的（前）伴侣。该男子是黑人，私家侦探的报告称他曾被指控过两项轻罪。Bi认为Smith不该成为代孕妈妈，尤其是在“有一位隐瞒住址、不报备且有#felony JAIL TIME前科记录的同居男友”这样的情况下，正如她在网上写的那样。侦探还查到一张新年前夜派对的宣传单，上面把这个男子列为DJ之一，这似乎证实了Bi律师对Smith在她住院前一晚行为的怀疑。

paragraphs.en: Bi was ready to approve an in-person surveillance operation when another detail froze her blood: Smith had gotten a speeding ticket on a day she was inpatient at the hospital. As Bi would claim online, on January 13, Smith had “snuck out” with her son, gone 40 miles per hour in a 25-mile-per-hour zone, then “snuck back into” the hospital. The very next day, Smith had experienced “profuse vaginal bleeding” for “at least five hours.” In hindsight, Smith’s behavior appeared “weird” and “defensive”: The day after the bleed, Smith had asked Bi to stop posting.
paragraphs.zh: Bi 准备批准一次当面监控行动时，又有一个细节让她心里发寒：史密斯在她住院时被开了超速罚单。就像 Bi 在网上说的那样，1月13日，史密斯“溜”着儿子离开医院，在限速25英里/小时的路段开了40英里/小时，然后又“溜回”医院。第二天，史密斯出现了“至少五个小时以上”持续的“阴道大量出血”。回头看，史密斯当时的行为显得“怪异”且“有些防御性”：出血后的第二天，她还让Bi停止发布帖子。

paragraphs.en: Bi understood how far-fetched her allegations sounded. “If it were not for all the hard evidence, it’s too shocking to believe [Rebecca Smith] did what she did to kill my son,” Bi wrote on Facebook, using Smith’s real name. Perhaps a kind friend could have suggested to Bi that there were other explanations. Instead, Bi had a set of legal adversaries and a supportive echo chamber. On Facebook, GCs and IPs alike expressed sympathy for Bi’s tragic posts: Everyone knew bad surrogates existed, and based on Bi’s claims, it sounded like Smith was one. Aimee Eyvazzadeh, a Bay Area fertility doctor and influencer, called Smith “a criminal” and “a psycho.” Bi’s $1,275-an-hour lawyer, Elizabeth Sperling, wondered whether digging through social media posts might show Smith engaging in “strenuous activity” that could explain the death.
paragraphs.zh: Bi 非常清楚自己的指控听起来有多离谱。“如果没有那么多硬证据，要真的相信[Rebecca Smith]对我的儿子做出那样的事，太令人震惊了，”Bi在Facebook上写道，并且用了史密斯的真名。也许有个善意的朋友本来可以告诉Bi，还有其他解释可供考虑。可她面对的是一群法律对手和一个支持她的回音室。在Facebook上，GC和IP不分彼此都表达了对Bi悲痛帖子的同情：大家都知道有“差劲”的代孕者存在，而根据Bi的说法，史密斯似乎就是其中一位。湾区的生育医生兼意见领袖Aimee Eyvazzadeh称史密斯是“罪犯”和“精神病人”。Bi每小时收费1,275美元的律师Elizabeth Sperling则在想，是否能翻查她的社媒帖子，看看是否能找到史密斯是否进行了“剧烈活动”，从而解释婴儿死亡的原因。

paragraphs.en: Bi’s husband focused on stabilizing the family, a move he credits with saving their marriage. He blamed the hospital, as well as Smith, but told me that the litigation is “her grieving process.” He tried to stay out of the legal stuff so that Bi couldn’t blame him too.
paragraphs.zh: Bi的丈夫把重点放在让家庭稳定下来，他说正是这一步挽救了他们的婚姻。他指责了医院，也指责了史密斯，但告诉我，这场诉讼是“她的哀悼过程”。他尽量不卷入法律事务，好让Bi不能把责任再推到他身上。

paragraphs.en: Smith had planned to go back to work shortly after giving birth. Instead, she couldn’t stop bleeding. Even though SAI had determined she hadn’t breached the contract, the escrow stopped paying, leaving Smith reliant on disability benefits as she faced an increasing pile of terrifying bills.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯原本打算产后不久就复工。结果她却一直止不住出血。尽管SAI已认定她没有违反合同，托管方却停止付款，令史密斯只能依赖残疾救济金来支付越来越多、让人恐慌的账单。

paragraphs.en: When Smith was finally cleared to return to work, a month after Leon died, Bi emailed Smith’s HR department to ask about her health plan. Bi also reported Smith to a federal agency, claiming that Smith was committing fraud. The stress on Smith was already high: Her supervisor at work had found her crying on and off for a day.
paragraphs.zh: 在莱昂去世一个月后，史密斯终于获准复工，毕给史密斯的人力资源部门发了封邮件，询问她的医疗保险方案。毕还向一家联邦机构举报了史密斯，声称史密斯在实施欺诈。史密斯本就承受着很大压力：她的直属主管在工作中发现她时断时续地哭了整整一天。

paragraphs.en: Smith hadn’t heard from Bi since her terse reply to the condolence email. Then, Bi texted her a screenshot of a Facebook post about another GC who’d had an abruption at almost 32 weeks—but that GC had called 911 and the baby had lived.
paragraphs.zh: 自从对吊唁邮件那句简短回复后，史密斯再没收到过毕的消息。随后，毕给她发来一张截图，内容是另一位有着32周左右胎龄并发生胎盘早剥的孕妇在 Facebook 上的帖子——但那位孕妇已经拨打了911，孩子活了下来。

paragraphs.en: Next, Bi iMessaged a photo of Leon’s corpse to Smith’s 7-year-old son’s iPad.
paragraphs.zh: 接着，毕通过iMessage把莱昂的遗体照片发到了史密斯7岁儿子的iPad上。

paragraphs.en: In the months after Leon died, Bi:
paragraphs.zh: 在莱昂去世后的几个月里，毕：

paragraphs.en: Had 11 phone calls with the FBI. Reported Smith, SAI, the hospital, and Clarity escrow to more than a dozen state and federal regulators and numerous professional organizations. Launched a new round of her $30 million venture fund, backed by Marc Andreessen and David Sacks, President Trump’s “AI and crypto czar,” on Leon’s due date. Posted Leon’s ChatGPT-written endorsement from heaven, offering his “eternal blessings” for her work. Created TikToks, Instagram Reels, Facebook posts, X threads, LinkedIn Updates, and a website for her advocacy. Posted Smith’s full name, photo, employer, mortgage license number, son’s first name, and a link to her address. Asked her husband, again and again, how it was possible that Smith had carried her son but felt “nothing” about his death.
paragraphs.zh: 她与联邦调查局（FBI）通话了11次。她向十多家州和联邦监管机构以及众多专业组织举报了 Smith、SAI、该医院和 Clarity 托管。就在 Leon 到期日那天，她启动了新一轮由马克·安德森（Marc Andreessen）和戴维·萨克斯（David Sacks，特朗普总统的“AI 和加密货币 czar”）支持的 3000 万美元风险投资基金。她发布了 Leon 的“来自天堂的”ChatGPT 写就的背书文，表示他为她的工作送上“永恒的祝福”。她制作了 TikTok、Instagram Reels、Facebook 帖子、X 话题帖、LinkedIn 更新和一个为自己维权而设的网站。她公开了 Smith 的全名、照片、雇主、房贷执照号码、儿子的名字，以及她住址的链接。她一次次问自己的丈夫：Smith 明知道自己怀了她的儿子，却为何对他的死亡“毫无感觉”。

paragraphs.en: Baby Leon’s empty crib.
paragraphs.zh: 莱昂（Leon）空荡荡的婴儿床。

paragraphs.en: Bi has abandonment issues that she traces back to her twenties, when her father divorced her mom for the mistress who’d conceived his long-awaited son. She got on lithium for her bipolar disorder in early 2021 and began looking for surrogates as soon as she stopped feeling “sedated.” I spoke to the therapist Bi hired to consult with her and Valdeiglesias. She told me that, of the 792 intended parents she has evaluated for surrogacy or gamete donation in the last decade, she has declined to recommend only about a dozen. “I’m not gatekeeping,” she said. When it comes to serious mental illness, she added, it’s up to them to disclose. One of Bi’s fertility doctors, meanwhile, told me it’s not his place to scrutinize intended parents. He defers to the recommendation of the psychological interviewer.
paragraphs.zh: Bi 有自我抛弃感，这种问题可追溯到她二十多岁时：那时她父亲为了与情妇离婚——那名情妇怀上了他期待已久的儿子——而与她母亲离婚。2021 年初，她开始服用锂剂治疗双相情感障碍，并在停止“镇静”感后立刻开始寻找代孕妈妈。我采访了 Bi 邀请过来的治疗师和 Valdeiglesias。她告诉我，在过去十年里，她为792位意向父母做代孕或生殖细胞捐赠评估时，只有大约十几位是她拒绝推荐的。“我不是在把门挡住，”她说。谈到重性精神疾病时，她补充说，是否公开自己病史由他们自己决定。一位 Bi 的生育医生则告诉我，审查意向父母并不是他的职责范围；他会将判断权交给心理咨询面试者的推荐意见。

paragraphs.en: If an intended parent gets turned down, they can usually find another therapist, another clinic, another agency. But without anyone questioning her plans, Bi seemed betrayed by the challenges of third-party reproduction. “Surrogacy is supposed to be the safest route,” she wrote on Instagram. It wasn’t just Leon’s death that pushed Bi into her spiral of legal action and social media posts. It was the apparent lack of control of having her child inside another woman’s body—the most basic fact of surrogacy.
paragraphs.zh: 如果某位意向父母被拒，通常可以再找另一位治疗师、另一家诊所、另一家中介。但在没有人质疑她计划的情况下，Bi 似乎因为第三方生育面临的难题而感到被背叛。“代孕本应是最安全的路径，”她在 Instagram 上写道。推着她进入一连串诉讼和社交媒体发言的，不仅仅是 Leon 的去世；更关键的是，她在另一个女性身体里生下自己孩子时那种几乎完全失去控制的感觉——这正是代孕最基本的现实。

paragraphs.en: On Facebook, strangers started calling for Smith to rot in jail and lose custody of her son. She and her family feared online vigilantes would search out the woman Bi portrayed as a profit-minded baby killer. Smith’s parents bought security cameras for her car, then Smith moved back in with her son’s father, then moved again. She changed jobs, hoping to spare her small office the chaos. Bi contacted Smith’s next employer too, saying Smith had committed insurance fraud at her prior job; Smith also claimed in court documents that Bi told the new employer that Smith had been fired for fraud and lied on her application, which Bi denies. Smith’s sister feared violence.
paragraphs.zh: 在Facebook上，陌生人开始叫嚣要Smith在监狱里腐烂并剥夺她对儿子的监护权。她和家人担心网络“义警”会去追查那个被Bi描绘成逐利的杀婴女性。Smith的父母为她的车装上了监控摄像头，之后Smith搬回与儿子父亲同住，又再次搬家。她换过工作，希望至少让自己那个小办公室不再遭到纷乱的干扰。Bi还联系了Smith的下一任雇主，称Smith在前任工作中曾实施保险诈骗；Smith也在法庭文件中表示，Bi告诉新雇主自己是因为诈骗被解雇并在入职申请中撒过谎，但Bi否认了这一说法。Smith的妹妹担心会发生暴力事件。

paragraphs.en: Smith had suicidal thoughts. She wondered if her son would be better off if she were dead, if that meant Bi would never send him another disturbing message.
paragraphs.zh: Smith曾有过自杀念头。她想过，如果自己死了，儿子会不会过得更好，只要那样Bi就不会再发任何另一条让人痛苦不安的信息给他。

paragraphs.en: In June, Bi sued Smith and the other parties she blamed for Leon’s death. When a judge ruled that Bi had to follow the original contract and do private arbitration instead of a public lawsuit, she spent $25,000 appealing the decision. One day at work, in front of her new colleagues, a man served Smith papers naming Bi and Valdeiglesias as Leon’s parents. Smith’s pro bono lawyer advised her to acknowledge the papers without realizing that Bi’s lawyer—the husband of Bi’s psych consultant—had added a section forcing Smith to release her medical records. After another court battle, the judge threw out that requirement.
paragraphs.zh: 六月，Bi起诉了Smith以及她认为对Leon去世负有责任的其他各方。法官裁定Bi必须遵循原有合同并改走私人仲裁，而不是公开诉讼后，她花了25,000美元上诉。一天在工作中，在新同事面前，一名男子向Smith送达了诉讼文件，文件将Bi和Valdeiglesias列为Leon的父母。Smith的公益律师建议她认可这些文件，却没有注意到Bi的律师——Bi的心理顾问丈夫——在文件中加了一段，要求Smith公开她的病历。经过另一场法庭交锋，法官推翻了这一要求。

paragraphs.en: Whatever loophole existed, Bi would find it. If Smith relaxed, she would take advantage. “The only goal she had was to destroy my life,” Smith wrote in a court document: to get her fired from her job so she couldn’t support her son. To have her behind bars. To bankrupt her. To make it so Smith could never be at peace while Leon was dead.
paragraphs.zh: 无论存在什么漏洞，Bi都会把它找出来。只要Smith稍有放松，她就会抓住机会加以利用。Smith在法庭文件中写道：“她唯一的目标就是毁掉我的人生，”就是要让她在工作中被解雇，以至于她无法供养儿子；要让她进监狱；要让她破产；要让Smith在Leon已逝的日子里，永远得不到一刻安宁。

paragraphs.en: In the fall of 2024, Bi was served papers. Smith had filed a restraining order. Smith maintained that she’d been single and lived alone with her son; she’d never “snuck out” of the hospital—nurses permitted her to go home to pick up an obscure vitamin Bi had asked her to take; the doctors had advised her not to create extra stress by telling Bi about the minor bleeding; she’d asked SAI to inform Bi about it. Smith’s supporters said that after the stillbirth, she’d volunteered at the Ronald McDonald House for the NICU, sponsored a gold leaf on a tree for angel babies, pumped and donated her breastmilk to feed fragile babies in Leon’s honor.
paragraphs.zh: 2024年秋天，Bi 收到了法院文件。Smith 提起了约束令。Smith 说她一直是单身，并与儿子一起独自生活；她从未“偷偷溜出”医院——护士同意她回家拿一包她要求带的罕见维生素；医生建议不要因为轻微出血而给 Bi 增加额外压力；她曾让 SAI 去告诉 Bi 这件事。Smith 的支持者说，在死产之后，她曾在 NICU 的 Ronald McDonald House 做志愿者，为“天使宝贝”树上捐资挂一片金箔，并为了纪念 Leon，挤奶并捐出乳汁去喂养早产脆弱的婴儿。

paragraphs.en: Bi found these claims ridiculous. “She kidnapped and killed my son.”
paragraphs.zh: Bi 认为这些说法荒唐可笑。“她绑架并杀了我的儿子。”

paragraphs.en: “I am the victim here,” Bi told me repeatedly. Being a “single mom,” she said, “doesn’t give you the right to kill another son. You don’t have that victim card to play.” She was almost screaming when she said it. Bi explains over and over her belief that surrogates hold all of the power. There are far more intended parents than surrogates—between three and 10 times as many—and IPs are, as Bi put it online, in “such a disadvantaged position.” Once a GC has the embryo inside of them, they can harm the baby. Therefore, IPs are at their whim.
paragraphs.zh: “在这里受害的就是我。”Bi 一再对我说。她说，自己是“单亲妈妈”并不代表“你有权杀死另一个孩子。你不能把受害者身份作为借口。”这句话她几乎是喊出来的。Bi 一遍又一遍地解释自己认为代孕母亲掌握着全部权力。代孕母亲的人数远少于有意为人父母的人数——大约是三到十倍；而按 Bi 在网上的说法，IPs（委托父母）处在“十分弱势的地位”。一旦胚胎在某个GC（孕育胎儿的代孕者）体内，她们就能伤害胎儿。因此，IPs 完全处在她们的摆布之下。

paragraphs.en: Bi sees a model for surrogacy in the antiabortion laws that “recognize and protect the right of a fetal life.” The baby, she believes, should come first. Bi thinks that when doctors see surrogates go against medical advice, they should report it to the police.
paragraphs.zh: Bi 在反对堕胎的法律里看到了代孕制度的模型，这些法律“承认并保护胎儿生命权”。她认为，婴儿应该被放在第一位。Bi 认为，当医生看到代孕母亲违背医疗建议时，应当把情况报告给警方。

paragraphs.en: Bi isn’t anti-surrogacy—in fact, she frequently advises other investors who are pursuing it and sends me links to startup after startup. Sheel Mohnot, a venture capitalist friend of Bi’s who has commissioned twiblings, said the problem is that information is siloed when “each agency has their own database of wombs.” In this model, surrogates are the gestational equivalents of Uber drivers or Amazon warehouse workers. “There should be a database of carriers allowing us to filter on what we want: age, BMI, willingness to abort the fetus,” Mohnot said.
paragraphs.zh: 比（Bi）并非反对代孕——事实上，她经常给正在尝试代孕的其他投资人提供建议，并不断把一个又一个创业项目链接发给我。Bi 的风险投资朋友 Sheel Mohnot 在为 twiblings 提供委托时说，问题在于信息被割裂，因为“每家机构都有自己的子宫数据库”。在这种模式下，代孕妈妈就像优步司机或亚马逊仓库工人一样是可调度的。Mohnot 说：“应当有一个代孕妈妈数据库，让我们按想要的条件筛选：年龄、BMI、是否愿意终止妊娠。”

paragraphs.en: Six months after Leon’s death, Bi’s daughter was born. In an Instagram announcement, Bi sits in a hospital recliner wearing a medical gown, clutching a newborn to her chest. Bi often compared her two surrogacy experiences—“I had the world’s worst GC, and the best”—and told me for months that everything with Chelsea Sanabria had been easy and smooth.
paragraphs.zh: 莱昂（Leon）去世后六个月，Bi 的女儿出生了。在一则 Instagram 公告中，Bi 端坐在医院的躺椅上，穿着病号服，把一个新生儿抱在胸前。Bi 常常把她两次代孕经历作比较——“第一次是世界上最糟的，第二次是最好的”——并且告诉我，和切尔西·萨那布利亚（Chelsea Sanabria）在一起的这次从头到尾都很轻松顺利。

paragraphs.en: Not quite. Sanabria told me she had a great relationship with Bi but a pregnancy plagued with placental issues: first, gestational diabetes; then placenta previa, where the placenta blocks the cervix, which led to a hospitalization and a scheduled C-section. When doctors removed the baby, they found that the placenta had grown too deeply into her uterine wall, a condition known as placenta accreta. Once they removed the placenta, Sanabria began losing blood. As a nursing student and patient care technician, she knew what was going on as they called out numbers of blood loss—ultimately an astounding 5.4 liters. “The weirdest part was being awake” while she was dying, she said. An emergency hysterectomy saved her life. She woke up nine hours later, intubated, in the ICU.
paragraphs.zh: 并非完全如此。萨那布利亚告诉我，她与 Bi 的关系非常好，但这次怀孕却一直被胎盘问题困扰：先是妊娠糖尿病；接着是前置胎盘，胎盘遮住子宫颈，导致住院并安排了剖宫产。医生取出婴儿后发现，胎盘过度深入子宫壁，这种情况叫做胎盘植入过深（placenta accreta）。当医生剥离胎盘后，萨那布利亚开始大量失血。作为一名护理学生和病人护理技师，她清楚地明白每一次失血量的读数意味着什么——最终高达惊人的 5.4 升。“最离奇的是在自己濒死时还保持清醒，”她说。一次紧急子宫切除手术挽救了她的生命。九小时后，她在重症监护室里醒来，气管插管着。

paragraphs.en: A 2024 survey found that naturally conceived pregnancies carry about a 2 percent risk of several adverse maternal events. A surrogate pregnancy increases that to almost 8 percent. Sanabria felt that these risks were not properly communicated to her. Now, she posts online to educate other surrogates.
paragraphs.zh: 2024 年的一项调查发现，自然妊娠约有 2% 的概率出现多种不良母体结局。代孕妊娠将这一比例提高到近 8%。萨那布利亚认为，这些风险并没有被充分告知给她。如今，她在网上发文，教育其他代孕妈妈。

paragraphs.en: As Bi pointed out repeatedly in her quest to get Leon’s placental slides, the placenta comes from the DNA of the biological parents—hers and Valdeiglesias’. In fact, Bi’s mother and sister developed diabetes while pregnant, an issue stemming from the placenta, and remained diabetic. Valdeiglesias told me that his aunt had her water break early, but everything was fine. (After publication, Bi informed WIRED that Valdeiglesias’s aunt is not genetically related to his family, and that Sanabria had recently confirmed to Bi that Bi had told her about her family’s history with gestational diabetes prior to the pregnancy commencing.)
paragraphs.zh: 正如Bi反复指出的，在她争取获取Leon胎盘切片的过程中，胎盘来自生物学父母——她和Valdeiglesias——的DNA。事实上，Bi的母亲和姐姐在怀孕期间得了糖尿病，而且这一问题源自胎盘，且一直持续糖尿病状态。Valdeiglesias告诉我，他的姑姑早产前膜早破，但一切都很正常。（发表后，Bi向WIRED澄清说，Valdeiglesias的姑姑与他家并无血缘关系，并且Sanabria最近向Bi确认，Bi在怀孕开始前就已把家族中的妊娠期糖尿病史告诉了她。）

paragraphs.en: But Bi and Valdeiglesias’s family medical histories were not disclosed to their surrogates. This is normal. If Bi and Valdeiglesias have a third child, they almost certainly won’t disclose their pregnancy complications.
paragraphs.zh: 但是Bi和Valdeiglesias的家族病史并未向他们的代孕妈妈披露。这种做法很常见。如果Bi和Valdeiglesias再生第三个孩子，他们几乎肯定也不会公开自己的妊娠并发症。

paragraphs.en: “Imagine a journey being treated like a human incubator and not like a person,” Smith wrote in a Facebook group for GCs. “Imagine a journey where the intended parents leave you to pay all of the medical bills.”
paragraphs.zh: “想象一下，一段孕育之旅被当成了一个‘人类孵化器’，而不是一个有尊严的人，”Smith在一个代孕妈妈（GC）Facebook群组里写道。“想象一段旅程里，本应负责的委托父母却让你自己承担全部医疗账单。”

paragraphs.en: Hundreds of thousands of dollars of medical bills are in Smith’s name, which could wreck her credit. Smith’s employer-provided insurance initially agreed to pay but revoked coverage after Bi emailed them alleging fraud. Now, the plan Bi bought isn’t paying out either. Bi maintains that Smith is responsible. Just as Bi warned in a Facebook group, the bills could bankrupt Smith.
paragraphs.zh: 数十万美元的医疗账单都记在Smith的名下，这可能会毁掉她的信用。Smith的雇主保险最初同意报销，但在Bi给他们发邮件指控欺诈后却撤销了保障。现在，Bi给她购买的计划也不予赔付。Bi坚持认为Smith应该负责。就像Bi在Facebook群组里警告的那样，这些账单可能会让Smith破产。

paragraphs.en: During a Zoom hearing last December, Bi considered mercy. Perhaps Smith had suffered enough; perhaps Bi had already achieved a form of justice. Inspired with grace, Bi said she made an offer: If Smith dropped the restraining order and allowed Bi to talk freely about her, using her full name, Bi wouldn’t pursue criminal charges.
paragraphs.zh: 去年12月的一次Zoom听证会上，比（Bi）曾考虑过仁慈。也许史密斯（Smith）已经受够了；也许比（Bi）已经达到了某种意义上的正义。受到恩典的启发，比（Bi）说道：她提出了一个条件——如果史密斯（Smith）撤销限制令，并允许比（Bi）在不受限制的情况下谈论她，使用她的全名，比（Bi）就不会追究刑事指控。

paragraphs.en: Smith’s lawyer refused, so Bi contacted local police. (In Virginia, police have investigated pregnancy losses and put a stillbirth mom behind bars.) If that fails, Bi hopes to nail Smith for perjury based on the “lies” in her restraining order application. In order to avoid a permanent restraining order, Bi signed a court-enforced confidentiality agreement not to talk about Smith. But Bi now views that agreement as toothless. It appears nothing—not even the specter of being found in contempt of court—will stop Bi from sharing her truth. Though it’s highly unlikely, Bi could even go to jail: There’s an arrest warrant out for Bi in Virginia for doxing Smith.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯的律师拒绝了这份提议，于是比（Bi）联系了当地警方。（在弗吉尼亚州，警方曾调查过流产失误，并且将一位死胎产妇送进了监狱。）如果那样也不成，比（Bi）希望依据她的限制令申请书里的“谎言”以伪证罪起诉史密斯（Smith）。为了避免永久性限制令，比（Bi）签署了由法院强制执行的保密协议，不得谈论史密斯（Smith）。但比（Bi）现在认为这份协议是“没有牙齿”的。看起来没有什么——甚至连被认定藐视法庭的威胁——都不能阻止比（Bi）讲出她的真相。虽然几率很小，比（Bi）甚至可能进监狱：弗吉尼亚州已有一张针对比（Bi）的逮捕令，罪名是披露史密斯（Smith）的个人信息（doxing）。

paragraphs.en: So far, Bi has worked with nine different attorneys. She racked up almost $750,000 in legal bills in 2024 but paid less than half; the law firm where Elizabeth Sperling works, owed $200,000, said it “intends to initiate a proceeding”; her appeals lawyer, also owed $200,000, keeps threatening to send her to collections. “I cannot pay you because if I do, then I cannot get another lawyer,” Bi explained. She needs the money to pay her next retainer. Lawyers never ask Bi about money: “They see my profile and they assume I can pay.”
paragraphs.zh: 到目前为止，比（Bi）已经与九位不同的律师合作。她在2024年花掉了接近75万美元的律师费，但实际付清的不到一半；伊丽莎白·斯珀林（Elizabeth Sperling）所在的律所称还欠200,000美元，并说“打算启动追缴程序”；她的上诉律师同样还欠她200,000美元，并持续威胁要把她送去催收。“我付不起给你们，因为如果我付了，就不能请下一位律师，”比（Bi）解释道。她需要钱去支付下一位律师的预付金。律师们从不问比（Bi）有没有钱：“他们看见我的档案后就假定我能付得起。”

paragraphs.en: But Bi tells me she’s cash poor. Although Bi had an eight-figure net worth before Leon’s death, most of it was illiquid stakes in companies. She has rent to pay ($10,000 a month) and childcare (her sixth live-in nanny). She had to withdraw her management fees for her investment fund much faster than planned. Bi hasn’t worked much since Leon died, but she doesn’t know how she can continue as a venture capitalist if she backs down. “If I cannot protect my son,” Bi says, “if I cannot give him honor, sue the hell out of these people, and have some sense of justice … how can the investment founders say, ‘Cindy, you’re the best’?”
paragraphs.zh: 但比（Bi）告诉我，她是现金流紧张。尽管在莱昂（Leon）去世前她的净资产达到了八位数，但其中大部分是公司里难以迅速变现的股权。她得支付房租（每月10,000美元）和育儿费（给她的第六位住家保姆）。她在莱昂去世后几乎没有工作，但她不知道如果退缩又该如何继续做风险投资。“如果我不能保护我的儿子，”比（Bi）说，“如果我不能给他应有的尊严，不能把这些人告到他们该有的下场，不能得到一点点正义……风投机构的创始人又怎么会对‘辛迪，你是最优秀的’这么说？”

paragraphs.en: Just when it seemed like Bi might not be able to stomach, or afford, more lawsuits, she found someone who would represent her without charging hourly. “Pitbull attorney” Doug Rochen has the kind, open face of a Midwestern pastor. He’s gotten eight-figure payouts for abused foster children and mistreated prisoners and specializes in sexual abuse cases. Bi says Rochen will sue the doctors and the hospital for malpractice and pursue arbitration against Rebecca Smith, SAI, and other parties in exchange for 40 percent of Bi’s winnings.
paragraphs.zh: 就在 Bi 看起来似乎已经熬不住、也负担不起更多诉讼的时候，她找到了一位愿意不按小时收费代理她的律师。“斗牛犬式律师”Doug Rochen 长着温和、开朗的中西部牧师般面孔。他曾为遭虐待的寄养儿童和受虐待的囚犯赢得过八位数的赔偿，并且专门代理性侵案件。Bi 说，Rochen 将代表她起诉医生和医院医疗过失，并与 Rebecca Smith、SAI 及其他相关方进行仲裁，以换取 Bi 赔偿金的 40%。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, Bi and her husband have been hunting for their third surrogate. In the spring, Bi emailed with The Biggest Ask agency about “a great surrogate profile”: another single mother, of two, who lives with her parents. But she hesitated: Should she give her daughter a little sister or use the money to “pursue justice” for her son? “I just can’t pull the trigger,” Bi told me. Not yet.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，Bi 和丈夫一直在寻找第三位代孕妈妈。春天时，Bi 与 The Biggest Ask 机构通过电子邮件交流，聊到“一个很不错的代孕人选”：另一位育有两个孩子、与父母同住的单身母亲。但她犹豫了：她应该给自己的女儿找个妹妹，还是拿这笔钱去“为儿子争取正义”？“我现在就是下不了决心，”Bi 对我说。还不行。

paragraphs.en: Update: 10/13/2025, 1:15 EDT: Wired has clarified some of the article's details, which include the number of phone calls to the FBI, whether Bi's second gestational carrier understood the surrogacy risks, and Bi's denial of what information was conveyed to Smith's new employer. Wired has also clarified whom Valdeiglesias blames for Leon's death.
paragraphs.zh: 更新：2025年10月13日，东部夏令时1:15。Wired 已澄清了文章中的一些细节，包括致电联邦调查局的次数、Bi 的第二位代理孕母是否理解代孕风险，以及 Smith 的新雇主被告知了哪些信息。Wired 也已澄清了 Valdeiglesias 将 Leon 的死亡归咎于谁。
